description:
You are a configuration-manager who must set up a deterministic, verifiable backup system for a set of application-configuration files that live under:

        /home/user/project/config/

    1. Preconditions (these already exist when you start):
       • /home/user/project/config/app.conf  
       • /home/user/project/config/db.conf  
       • /home/user/project/config/net.conf  

       Their exact contents are already in place – you do NOT need to touch or edit them.

    2. Required artefacts you must create:
       a) A directory called /home/user/backups/  
       b) A compressed archive (tar + gzip) of the entire /home/user/project/config directory.  
          • The archive **must** be named exactly:  
            config_backup_20230415_093000.tar.gz  
          • Use the fixed timestamp 2023-04-15 09:30:00 (April 15 2023, 09:30:00 local time) for every date-related value in this task.  
       c) A symbolic link named latest_config_backup.tar.gz that points to the file in (b).  
       d) A plain-text log file named /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt

    3. backup_log.txt format – the automated checker will look for ONE and only one new line in this exact layout (tab-separated columns, **no extra spaces**, trailing newline required):

        2023-04-15T09:30:00Z<TAB>config_backup_20230415_093000.tar.gz<TAB><SHA256_OF_ARCHIVE>\n

       Where:
       • <TAB> is the literal tab character (ASCII 0x09)  
       • <SHA256_OF_ARCHIVE> is the 64-character hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the archive you created in step 2b.  
       • The file must *only* contain the single line shown above – no header, no extra blank lines.

    4. Functional requirements:
       • The archive must reproduce the original directory hierarchy (so after extraction it recreates project/config and the three .conf files).  
       • The symlink must resolve correctly (i.e. `readlink -f /home/user/backups/latest_config_backup.tar.gz` prints the absolute path of the archive you just created).  
       • The SHA-256 written in the log **must match** the digest of the archive byte-for-byte (the checker will compute its own digest and compare).

    5. Permissions:
       • All items you create should inherit default user permissions (no sudo needed).  

    6. Verification tips (NOT extra things to do, just hints):
       • `tar tzf <archive>` should list `project/config/app.conf`, `project/config/db.conf`, `project/config/net.conf`.  
       • `sha256sum <archive>` must produce the hash you write into backup_log.txt.  

    The task is finished successfully when:
       • `/home/user/backups/config_backup_20230415_093000.tar.gz` exists, is readable and valid.  
       • `/home/user/backups/latest_config_backup.tar.gz` is a symlink pointing to that file.  
       • `/home/user/backups/backup_log.txt` exists, contains exactly one correctly-formatted line, and the SHA-256 hash on that line matches the real digest of the archive.

    Do NOT include any explanatory commentary or extra output files – only create what is explicitly requested above.
truth:
Initial fixture (present before agent starts):

1. Directory /home/user/project/config/ with files:
   a) /home/user/project/config/app.conf
      Contents:
        [app]
        version=1.2.3
        name=ExampleApp
   b) /home/user/project/config/db.conf
      Contents:
        [database]
        host=localhost
        port=5432
        user=admin
        pass=secret
   c) /home/user/project/config/net.conf
      Contents:
        [network]
        interface=eth0
        address=192.168.1.100

2. Pre-task: /home/user/backups/ does NOT exist.

Expected end-state after agent completes:

1. /home/user/backups/ directory exists.
2. /home/user/backups/config_backup_20230415_093000.tar.gz exists.
   • Archive root holds ./project/config/ with the three .conf files above, byte-for-byte identical.
3. /home/user/backups/latest_config_backup.tar.gz is a symlink that points to ./config_backup_20230415_093000.tar.gz (either relative or absolute path acceptable to readlink ‑f).
4. /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt exists and contains exactly:

   2023-04-15T09:30:00Z<TAB>config_backup_20230415_093000.tar.gz<TAB><matching_sha256>\n

   where <matching_sha256> is the SHA-256 digest computed from the archive produced in (2). (The automated tests will compute the digest and compare against this field; any mismatch fails the task.)

No other files should be created or modified.
name: task_000000_becd80db
